injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Returned to availability in mid-May 2026; expected fully fit for the tournament

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Returned to availability for Forest after missing four games with a minor issue

injuryUpdates.source: Transfermarkt injury list, RotoWire, 3 Added Minutes - May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Forest manager Vitor Pereira said the issue was 'not serious, it was just a small problem' and Ndoye returned to availability in mid-May after missing four club games. Predicted starting wide forward for Switzerland, so worth monitoring through the May 31 Jordan friendly.

bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Switzerland are the +100 favorites to top Group B ahead of Canada (+210), Bosnia and Herzegovina (+350) and Qatar (+2200). Tournament outright shortened slightly from 100/1 to roughly +6000 (~80/1) across major US sportsbooks. Dedicated group-stage-exit price not publicly listed by the aggregators consulted.

summary: Quiet four-week window for Switzerland: no international fixtures since the late-March Germany and Norway friendlies, no coach change, and the final 26-man squad will not be revealed by Murat Yakin until May 20 (after this scout date). Only meaningful delta is a minor club-level fitness issue for predicted starting forward Dan Ndoye, who missed four Forest games but was passed fit again in mid-May. Bookmakers continue to price the Nati as clear Group B favorites at +100, with tournament outright around +6000.
